about.title: About
about.p1: I'm a Full-stack Developer focused on developing Websites and Web Applications that help clients succeed in their ventures. Check out some of the applications in the Projects section! See
about.p2: I also share my professional life with the community, so feel free to follow me on LinkedIn, where I post updates about my studies and professional career. I'm also available to answer any questions through the contact channels. See
about.p3: I'm open to job opportunities where I can contribute and learn. If you have a good opportunity that matches my skills and experiences, then don't hesitate to contact me.
about.p4: Here you can see more about my previous experiences:
about.p5: I have experience with various technologies and I am always updating myself and learning new things to help provide the best possible solution for the problems that arise. Check out the technologies I have been working with on

blog.subtitle: Studies, curiosities and technologies
blog.p-1: On my blog I share articles about the technologies I study and use on a daily basis, such as TypeScript, Node.js, software architecture, DevOps and more. The goal is to document my learning and contribute to the dev community, bringing practical and straight-to-the-point content.
blog.p-2: You will also find curiosities about the world of technology, career tips and reflections on the process of evolving as a developer. If you enjoy technical content with a touch of real experience, feel free to explore!

posts.event-loop: Understanding the Event Loop in Node.js
posts.docker: Docker from scratch for backend devs

linkedin.p-1: I am a full-stack developer with experience in TypeScript, JavaScript, HTML, CSS, React, Next.js, and Node.js. I am passionate about building scalable, intuitive, and high-performance web applications, always striving to adopt best practices and explore new technologies. Beyond development, I have a strong interest in software architecture, performance optimization, and cloud service integration.
linkedin.p-2: On my LinkedIn, I share knowledge, experiences, and insights from my professional journey. I am always open to new connections, collaborations, and opportunities to grow in the field. If you'd like to discuss technology or development, feel free to send me a message!

github.p-1: On my GitHub, you will find projects I have developed using TypeScript, JavaScript, HTML, CSS, React, Next.js, and Node.js. I enjoy building modern web applications, exploring best development practices, clean architecture, and performance optimization. Additionally, I am always looking to learn new technologies and improve my skills through practical challenges.
github.p-2: I also share repositories with solutions for everyday problems, experiments with libraries and frameworks, as well as contributions to the community. If you’d like to check out my code, give feedback, or collaborate on a project, feel free to explore my repositories!

technologies.title: My Technologies

projects.title: Projects
projects.subtitle: A selection of things I've built

streaming.description: Microservice architecture for video processing and streaming. The upload service (Node.js/Express) supports chunked multipart uploads with per-part retry logic and the ability to resume an interrupted upload at any point — making it resilient to network failures and crashes. Video processing runs in a separate Golang service, communicating via RabbitMQ. Redis is used for upload state tracking.
highlights: Chunked multipart upload with retry per part
Resume upload from any point after failure

sphera.description: Full-featured educational platform for students seeking to study abroad. Built with scalability in mind — async job processing for notifications and heavy tasks, Redis caching for performance, and deployed on AWS infrastructure.
highlights: Async job queues for background processing

vscode.description: Personal portfolio built as an interactive replica of the Visual Studio Code interface. Every detail is intentional — draggable tabs, collapsible file explorer, working minimap, integrated terminal with real commands, and keyboard shortcuts.

gorouter.description: A REST API in Go built from scratch with zero HTTP frameworks. The router is implemented manually using only the standard net/http package and regular expressions for route matching — including named path parameters and method dispatching. A deep dive into how frameworks like Gin and Echo work under the hood.

guide.title: Site Guide
guide.p1: This portfolio was developed with inspiration from Visual Studio Code (VS Code). If you are not familiar with VS Code or want to see my other portfolio, just visit:
guide.subtitle: Features:
guide.p2: This site is fully interactive, responsive, and all icons are clickable
guide.p3: Navigate the page using the minimap in the upper right corner (just click on it to scroll up or down the page)
guide.p4: Access different parts of the site through the left side menu
guide.p5: Open and close the file explorer by clicking again on the selected icon in the side menu
guide.p6: Expand and collapse directories within the file explorer
guide.p7: Manage tabs: open, close, and rearrange by dragging on the top bar
guide.p8: Switch between languages using the icon:
guide.footer: I will always be updating this portfolio whenever I have time, so feel free to come back anytime to explore and learn more about my work!
guide.terminal-p1: Open and close the terminal with Ctrl+J or the terminal icon in the left sidebar
guide.terminal-p2: Drag the top edge of the terminal to resize it
guide.terminal-p3: Type clear to clear the terminal output
guide.terminal-p4: Use the arrow keys ↑ ↓ to navigate through command history
